finding_results.notes: Revised both cited thesis-speeches. At line 439, the balanced 'He says route / You say precinct' parallel is now interrupted by an em-dash self-correction ('makes you—it's the same thing. It's the same damn thing'), breaking the neat parallel into a pressure repetition with profanity; the ending drops the tidy 'call it handled' formulation for the rougher 'you're both just standing here shoving it in a drawer.' At line 525, the preamble thesis ('This is what we do on this ship') is removed and replaced with an aborted summary after the three-part list ('That's just—'), showing Faye starting to name the pattern and failing to land the formulation before pivoting to the concrete consequence. Both revisions preserve anger, specificity, and high-contraction register while converting composed thesis-speech into pressure-roughened dialogue with visible interruptions. The two cited speeches no longer form consecutive polished parallel-clause summaries.

summary: Addressed the single finding (chapter_11_dialogue_01) by roughening Faye's two cited thesis-speeches at lines 439 and 525. At 439, the parallel 'He says / You say' structure is broken by an em-dash self-interruption and pressure repetition. At 525, the preamble thesis is replaced by an aborted summary ('That's just—') after the three-part list. No consecutive polished summary lines remain in the cited exchanges. Changes are minimal and dialogue-local; heading contract preserved, no structural or continuity modifications.
